Regions Artists Timeline All works

Edvard Munch

29 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
The Scream
by Edvard Munch
Expressionism · Edvard Munch · 1893
Munch wrote in his diary: "I sensed an infinite scream passing through nature." The sky's red may be from the Krakatoa eruption ten years earlier.
The Girl by the Window
by Edvard Munch
Norway · Post-Impressionism · 1893 · Oil on canvas
Consolation
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1894 · Drypoint and open bite in black on cream wove paper
Harpy
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1894 · Drypoint in black ink on heavyweight off-white wove plate paper
The Girl at the Window
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1894 · Drypoint
Summer Night (The Voice)
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1895 · etching and plate roughened with sandpaper
Madonna
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1895–1896 · Handcolored lithograph in black on mottled gray-blue wove paper (discolored to gray-green)
The Hands
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1895 · Lithograph in black on cream card
Moonlight. Night in St Cloud
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1895 · Drypoint
Evening, Melancholy I
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1896 · Hand-colored woodcut
The Sick Child I
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1896 · Transfer lithograph printed from two stones in pale blue and black ink on ivory wove paper
Boys Bathing
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1896 · Color burnished aquatint
Young Woman on the Beach
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1896 · Burnished aquatint and drypoint in purple
Attraction I
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1896 · Lithograph in black ink on grayish-ivory China paper
Stéphane Mallarmé
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1897 · Transfer lithograph with crayon and scraping in green on cream Japanese paper
The Fat Whore
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1897 · Woodcut with gouges
Two Women on the Shore
by Edvard Munch
Norway · Post-Impressionism · 1898 · Woodcut printed from one block (sawn into three sections) in dark blue
Man Bathing
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1899 · Color woodcut from two blocks on grayish ivory card
Woman's Head against the Shore
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1899 · Woodcut printed from two blocks (each sawn into two sections) in light green
Woman with Red Hair and Green Eyes (The Sin)
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1901 · color lithograph
The Dead Mother and Her Child
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1901 · Etching with open bite and drypoint in black ink on heavyweight cream wove paper
From Max Linde's House: Theodor Linde
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1902 · drypoint
The Kiss IV
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1902 · Woodcut printed from two blocks (one sawn into two sections) in gray-brown and black ink on cream Japanese paper
Woman with Red Hair and Green Eyes. The Sin
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1902 · Transfer lithograph printed from two stones in yellow

by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1903 · Lithograph on thin Japanese paper

by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1908–9 · lithograph

by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1908–9 · lithograph
Alpha and Omega
by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1908–1909 · Lithograph on paper

by Edvard Munch
Norway · 1918 · Woodcut in blue and lithograph (from multiple stones) in yellow
